3.narrative: Ethereum started the period at a price of 3040.2078 and ended at 1974.1379, reflecting a negative return of -35.0657%. The asset experienced significant volatility with an annualized volatility of 0.629596 and a maximum drawdown of -45.698%. Among the largest daily moves, the biggest upward session occurred on 2026-02-06, with a 13.2682% gain closing at 2063.387 and accompanied by very high volume of 64998454203, which was also tagged as a high volume day. This rally coincided with multiple news items including Ethereum daily market analysis and price predictions, as well as reports of a pause in a recurring market dump after a lawsuit, suggesting some positive market sentiment around that date [1][10]. Conversely, the largest down day was on 2026-02-05, with a -15.0138% drop closing at 1821.6831 and similarly high volume of 60381662655, also tagged as a high volume day. This sharp decline occurred amid headlines about SEC probes into Ethereum's security status and notable sales of ETH by a co-founder, which may have contributed to negative sentiment [7][9]. Another notable down day was on 2026-01-31 with a -9.5206% move. Average daily volume increased by 4.4605% to approximately 24 billion, indicating heightened trading activity during this period. Additionally, on 2026-02-25, Ethereum saw a 10.883% gain closing at 2054.6279 with volume of 27717195805, coinciding with reports of $157 million net inflows into Ethereum spot ETFs, which may have supported the price increase [2]. Overall, the period was marked by large price swings and elevated volumes, influenced by regulatory developments, market inflows, and notable trading activity.
tldr: Highest volume spike was on 2026-02-06 with volume 64,998,454,203.
3.conclusion: Over this period, the asset ended lower (-35.07%) with peak drawdown of -45.70%, indicating meaningful price swings during the window.
